hub_subtitle: Conversação para viagens, listening de verdade e reuniões internacionais — rumo a Nova York, setembro de 2026

lesson.menu_title: If I Miss My Flight -- Handling Problems, Contingencies, and Unexpected Situations
lesson.menu_desc: Voo cancelado em Miami: remarcar, pedir voucher e resolver a noite em inglês -- 38 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 4 -- If I Miss My Flight
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Lucimara | Aula 4 -- If I Miss My Flight

listenings.text: Good evening, ladies and gentlemen. This is a gate announcement for flight fourteen eighty-seven to New York Kennedy. I am sorry to tell you that this flight has been canceled tonight because of the weather in the northeast. Please do not leave the gate area yet. If you have a meeting or a connection tomorrow morning, come to the desk and we will rebook you at no extra cost. If you booked through a travel agent, you will need to call your agent directly, because we cannot change that reservation here. Every passenger tonight will receive a meal voucher, and passengers with a long wait will also receive a hotel voucher. Unless you hear your name on this speaker, please stay in the line. We will start with the first twenty passengers, and we will get everyone out of Miami tonight or early tomorrow morning. Thank you for your patience.

Hello, this is Karen from the customer care team, returning your call about flight fourteen eighty-seven. I have good news and one condition. The good news first. I have already rebooked you through Charlotte, and you will land at Kennedy at six ten in the morning. Your seat is confirmed, and you will board here in Miami at five o'clock. Now the condition. You must check in again at the counter before four thirty, because a different airline operates the first part of the trip. If you are not at that counter by four thirty, the system will release your seat, and I cannot promise you anything after that. Your hotel is two minutes from the terminal by shuttle, and your meal voucher is already in your email. If anything changes tonight, call this number again and ask for me. Have a good rest.

reading.rtitle: The Night the Board Turned Red
paras: The board at Miami International turns red at seven o'clock in the evening. DELAYED. Twenty minutes later, one word worse: CANCELED. Lucimara is standing in front of it with a carry-on, a phone at eleven percent, and a meeting at nine the next morning in Manhattan.

The delay notice arrived on her phone an hour ago, but she did not read it -- she was in the air. Now there are sixty people in the line at the desk, and one agent behind it. She does a quick calculation. If she only waits in that line, she will be there for two hours. If she calls the airline while she waits, she will be in two lines at the same time. So she does both.

The agent is called Brian, and he is tired. The next direct flight is overbooked, he says, and the one after that is full too. "If you can fly through Charlotte, I can get you into JFK by six in the morning." It is not the itinerary she paid for. It is an alternative route, and it works. "As long as I am in Manhattan by eight, I will be fine," she says. Brian gives her a meal voucher and a hotel voucher -- not because she shouted, but because she asked, in a full sentence, and she asked early.

She ends up eating a bad sandwich at eleven at night, in a terminal she never planned to see. Her travel insurance will cover the hotel. Tomorrow she will file a complaint, and she may get compensation. But tonight only one thing matters: she sorted it out in English, and she will board at five in the morning.

gist.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Miami International is a badly organized airport.
When a trip breaks, the language you use in the first ten minutes decides how the night ends.
You should never fly through Charlotte in the winter.

items: The delay notice reached her phone before she landed in Miami.
"The delay notice arrived on her phone an hour ago, but she did not read it -- she was in the air."
She stayed in the line and did nothing else.
"If she calls the airline while she waits, she will be in two lines at the same time. So she does both."
Brian puts her on the same route she originally booked.
"It is not the itinerary she paid for. It is an alternative route" -- through Charlotte.
Brian gives her the vouchers because she asked early, in a full sentence.
"not because she shouted, but because she asked, in a full sentence, and she asked early."
The airline has already paid her compensation.
"Tomorrow she will file a complaint, and she may get compensation" -- it has not happened yet.

items.situation: The board says CANCELED. You are fifth in the line at the desk. Open the conversation.
tips: Good evening. My flight to New York has been canceled. What are my options tonight?
Ask first, complain later -- the first question decides the whole night.

items.situation: The agent says the next direct flight is overbooked. Offer him a condition.
tips: If you can get me into JFK before eight, I'll take any route.
As long as I land before eight, I'll be fine -- I don't need a direct flight.

items.situation: You will wait seven hours in Miami. Ask for what the airline owes you.
tips: Will I get a meal voucher and a hotel voucher for tonight?
Unless there is a seat tonight, I'll end up sleeping at the airport.

items.situation: Your suitcase did not come out with you in Charlotte.
tips: My bag hasn't arrived. If it doesn't arrive tonight, will you deliver it to my hotel?
Could you write down the hotel address for the delivery?

items.situation: You want the airline to pay for the night you lost.
tips: I'd like to file a complaint and request compensation. Where do I do that?
How long will it take if I file the complaint online?

items.situation: The hotel asks for a credit card, but you have travel insurance.
tips: My travel insurance covers the hotel. Could I have a receipt, so I can request a refund?
Could you put the voucher number on the receipt as well?
